title: Robotics: Giving Sight to Machines
concept: Robotic vision enables robotic arms and autonomous machines to perceive depth, navigate environments, and manipulate objects.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why is sensitivity (recall) prioritized in medical imaging models?

options.text: To avoid missing potential cases of disease (minimizing false negatives)
To ensure the model is compact enough for a phone

exercises.explain: High sensitivity ensures that diseases are not missed, which is critical for patient health.

cards.title: Perceiving the 3D world
cards.body: A blind robot is just a pre-programmed machine that repeats the same movement. But if you give it cameras, it can see! It can detect if an object has shifted, navigate around obstacles, and pick up packages of varying shapes.

cards.title: Robotic Vision & Depth Sensing
cards.body: Robotic vision combines object detection with depth perception (measuring distance). This is achieved using stereo cameras (two cameras mimicking human eyes), LiDAR (laser scanning), or RGB-D cameras.

cards.title: Closing one eye
cards.body: Try to touch two pencil tips together with one eye closed — it's difficult because you lose depth perception. Robots need special sensors or multiple cameras to perceive depth so they don't crash into things.

cards.title: Industrial pick-and-place
cards.body: A robotic arm in a warehouse uses an RGB-D camera. The AI detects the coordinates of box corners and calculates the exact depth (Z-axis distance), allowing the gripper to pick up the box smoothly.

cards.title: Robots don't just use standard camera frames
cards.body: You might think robots see the world as flat 2D images. In reality, modern robotic systems convert visual inputs into point clouds (millions of 3D data coordinates) to map physical space.

left.text:
Captures color and depth
Simulates binocular vision
Works well in lit rooms
Low cost

right.text:
Fires laser pulses
Measures travel reflection time
Works in pitch darkness
Highly accurate 3D maps

exercises.prompt: Which sensor uses laser pulses to measure distance and construct highly accurate 3D point maps?
exercises.explain: LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) uses lasers to calculate distances, creating detailed 3D maps.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Stereo cameras require two distinct lenses to estimate depth.
exercises.explain: True. By comparing the offset between the two images (disparity), the system calculates how far away objects are.

exercises.sentence: A collection of 3D data points representing the surface of an object in space is called a point ___.
exercises.explain: Point clouds represent the spatial coordinate data generated by LiDAR or depth sensors.

exercises.prompt: Why is depth perception critical for a robotic arm picking up boxes?

options.text: The arm needs to know the exact distance to the box to avoid crashing or missing it
Depth determines the color temperature of the packaging
It speeds up the database queries for inventory tracking

exercises.explain: Without depth data, a robot cannot locate objects in 3D space to grasp them.

exercises.prompt: You are building a self-driving delivery cart that must navigate busy city sidewalks at night. Which sensor setup provides the best collision avoidance in dark conditions?

options.text: A combination of LiDAR and active infrared depth sensors.
A single standard web camera without flash.
An image classification model trained on daytime photos.
No sensors; rely on pre-programmed coordinates.

exercises.explain: Active sensors like LiDAR and infrared cameras do not rely on ambient lighting, making them perfect for night-time navigation and obstacle avoidance.
